placeholder: Enter Thai stock ticker (e.g. PTT, AOT, CPALL)
placeholderUs: Enter US stock ticker (e.g. AAPL, TSLA, MSFT)
fetchError: Failed to fetch stock data. Please check the ticker and try again.
buyExample: At least 2 of 3 indicators are bullish. For example, SMA 20 crosses above SMA 50 (Golden Cross), MACD turns positive, or Stochastic exits oversold zone (<20). This suggests upward momentum — consider buying.
holdExample: Indicators are mixed — some bullish, some bearish. No strong conviction either way — wait for a clearer signal.
sellExample: At least 2 of 3 indicators are bearish. For example, SMA 20 crosses below SMA 50 (Death Cross), MACD turns negative, or Stochastic exits overbought zone (>80). This suggests downward momentum — consider selling.
noIndicators: No indicators selected. Toggle at least one indicator above to see a signal.
sensitivityHint: How many indicators must agree for a Buy or Sell signal
conservativeDesc: All active indicators must agree for a Buy or Sell
normalDesc: Buy/Sell when more indicators are bullish/bearish than not
aggressiveDesc: Any single indicator can trigger a Buy or Sell
watchlistEmpty: Search for a stock and click + to add it here
edu_sma_what: A Simple Moving Average (SMA) is the average closing price over a set number of days. The "crossover" strategy compares a short-term SMA (fast) to a long-term SMA (slow).
edu_sma_calc: SMA = (Sum of closing prices over N days) ÷ N. For example, SMA 20 adds up the last 20 closing prices and divides by 20.
edu_sma_read: When the short SMA crosses above the long SMA, it's called a Golden Cross — a bullish (buy) signal. When it crosses below, it's a Death Cross — a bearish (sell) signal. The wider the spread between them, the stronger the trend.
edu_sma_flip: This signal flips when the short SMA crosses the long SMA in the opposite direction.
edu_macd_what: Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) measures the relationship between two exponential moving averages (EMAs). It helps identify changes in momentum, direction, and strength of a trend.
edu_macd_calc: MACD Line = EMA(fast, e.g. 12) − EMA(slow, e.g. 26). Signal Line = EMA of the MACD Line (e.g. 9-period). Histogram = MACD Line − Signal Line. The histogram visualizes the gap between MACD and Signal.
edu_macd_read: When the MACD line crosses above the Signal line, momentum is shifting bullish (buy). When it crosses below, momentum is bearish (sell). A growing histogram means the trend is strengthening; a shrinking one means it's weakening.
edu_macd_flip: This signal flips when the MACD line crosses the Signal line in the opposite direction.
edu_stoch_what: The Stochastic Oscillator compares a stock's closing price to its price range over a period. It measures momentum and helps identify overbought or oversold conditions on a scale of 0–100.
edu_stoch_calc: %K = ((Current Close − Lowest Low) ÷ (Highest High − Lowest Low)) × 100 over a set period (e.g. 14 days). %D is a smoothed average of %K (e.g. 3-period SMA of %K).
edu_stoch_read: Above 80 = overbought (the stock may be due for a pullback). Below 20 = oversold (the stock may be due for a bounce). When %K crosses above %D in the oversold zone, it's a buy signal. When %K crosses below %D in the overbought zone, it's a sell signal.
edu_stoch_flip: This signal flips when %K crosses %D in the opposite direction, or when the oscillator moves out of the overbought/oversold zone.
edu_scoring_body: Each active indicator gives a score: +1 for Buy, 0 for Hold, −1 for Sell. These are added together. With Normal sensitivity, the majority must agree. Conservative requires all to agree. Aggressive allows any single indicator to trigger. The gauge position reflects the total score relative to the maximum possible.
bt_subtitle: See how your signal settings would have performed historically
bt_rule_normal: Buy when more indicators are bullish than bearish. Sell when more are bearish. Hold when tied. Using: [{ind}].
bt_rule_aggressive: Buy when ANY indicator in [{ind}] is bullish (even if others disagree). Sell when ANY is bearish.
bt_rule_conservative: Buy ONLY when ALL of [{ind}] agree bullish. Sell ONLY when ALL agree bearish. Very few trades.
bt_rule_none: No indicators selected. Enable at least one indicator in the Signals tab.
bt_no_trades_msg: Your current settings didn't produce any buy/sell signals in this period. Try a longer timeframe, switch to Aggressive sensitivity, or enable more indicators.
bt_rule_hold: Must hold at least {n} days before selling.
bt_rule_cooldown: Wait {n} days after selling before buying again.
bt_rule_confirm: Signal must persist for {n} consecutive days before acting.
alertsEmpty: No alerts yet. Create one to get notified when signals change or prices cross targets.
pt_no_positions: No open positions. Use the form above to paper-buy stocks.
